div1.entryFree:
Stray, subst. 1) dereliction, aberration: I would not from your love make such a s. Lr. I, 1, 212.
2) a straggler, a vagabond: impounded as a s. the king of Scots, H5 I, 2, 160. to seize me for a s. H6B IV, 10, 27.
Collectively, == stragglers: pursue the scattered s. H4B IV, 2, 120.
